comments.body: please create a minimal reproducible example before posting. Once all compilation errors are fixed, your code works fine: jdoodle.com/a/6h4
@Misha I created a minimal functional example, and it works too. I still can't get a grasp why doesn't it work on my original project.
the method you called from inside your script (getResult) probably just returned null and it had nothing to do with nashorn.

There are no package or public in JavaScript, and certainly no explicit typing, so are you sure the javascript tag applies here? Is the problem actually with the two lines of JS? And of course: I don't see any return statements in your JS, nor any console logging, so ... are you sure this code is supposed to return anything rather than just "run" and because there is no return statement, it doesn't return anything.

title: How to proper evaluate a small script with Nashorn?
body:
I have a JavaScript script simillar to the following

var base = 1
var next = myPackage.MyClass.getResult(base);

And the following class defined in package myPackage

public class MyClass{
  public static getResult(final int base){
    //Inner logic
  return result; //Returns another class defined by me
}

Now, the eval(String script, Bindings n) method of ScriptEngine returns supposedly the result of a script, such as where in the example

ScriptEngine engine;= new ScriptEngineManager().getEngineByName("nashorn");
bindings = new SimpleBindings();
engine.eval("1 + 1", bindings);

the eval call returns 2, I figured I could do something of the kind:

ScriptEngine engine;= new ScriptEngineManager().getEngineByName("nashorn");
bindings = new SimpleBindings();
String baseScript = "var base = 1; var result = myPackage.MyClass.getResult(base);"
String script = baseScript + "result.toString()";
Object result = engine.eval(script, bindings);

But the result of this is always null, although it evaluates correctly the getResult(base) method (if I place a breakpoint inside, I can detect the call is made and the logic done correctly).

Why is the result always null and how should this be done correctly?

Edit: full code example:

public class Main {
    public static void main (String[] args) throws java.lang.Exception
    {
        ScriptEngine engine = new ScriptEngineManager().getEngineByName("nashorn");
        SimpleBindings bindings = new SimpleBindings();
        String baseScript = "var base = 1; var result = Packages.myPackage.MyClass.getResult(base);";
        String script = baseScript + "result.toString()";
        Object result = engine.eval(script, bindings);
        System.out.println(result);
    }
}

package myPackage;
public class MyClass
{
    public static MyClass.ResultClass getResult(final int base) {
        return new MyClass.ResultClass().setString(base);
    }

    public static class ResultClass{
        private String str;

        public MyClass.ResultClass setString(int val){
            if(val < 10) {str = String.valueOf(val);}
            else str = "0";
            return this;
        }
        public String getString(){
            return str;
        }
    }
}
